steps.description: Assembly of [Et4N][Tp*WFe2Cl2S3(µ-SMe)] (WFe2) using the templating trisulfide anion [Tp*WS3]- (Tp* = tris(3,5-dimethyl-1-pyrazolyl)borate) following stepwise assembly of WFe and WFe2 clusters, closely related to ethylthiolate-bridged and chloride-bridged clusters in the literature. The complex features a WFe2S3 core in which the two iron sites are bridged by a µ2-thiolate group.

synthesis.notes: The compound WFe2 is described as closely related to ethylthiolate-bridged (ref 35) and chloride-bridged (ref 36) clusters in the literature. The methyl group in the crystallographic structure is disordered between endo and exo conformations. The 1H NMR spectrum shows a 2:1 ratio of Tp* methyl integrations indicating Cs symmetry with the mirror plane containing W, Ni, and the SMe group. Detailed synthetic procedure is not explicitly described in the paper text; the compound is prepared using the [Tp*WS3]- templating strategy developed by Holm, Xu, and Agapie for stepwise assembly of WFe, WFe2, and WFe3 clusters.

steps.description: Addition of IPrNi(styrene)2 (IPr = 1,3-bis(2,6-diisopropyl)-1,3-dihydro-2H-imidazol-2-ylidene) to [Et4N][Tp*WFe2Cl2S3(µ-SMe)] (WFe2) in a THF:MeCN mixture, resulting in conversion to a new Cs species as indicated by a shift in the two diagnostic Tp* resonances in the 1H NMR spectrum.

synthesis.notes: The product WFe2Ni-red has a Ni site bound to two sulfides and the NHC ligand, which is clearly three-coordinate as the Ni···SMe distance of 3.102(12) Å is too long to be a bond. The nickel-sulfide distances are 2.1681(8) and 2.1775(8) Å, while the nickel-carbon distance is 1.951(3) Å. The W–Ni distance of 2.6216(5) Å is classified as a single bond based on the formal shortness ratio of 1.07. IPr = 1,3-bis(2,6-diisopropyl)-1,3-dihydro-2H-imidazol-2-ylidene; Tp* = tris(3,5-dimethyl-1-pyrazolyl)borate. The compound has an S = 7/2 ground state and formal oxidation states Fe2.5+2W3+Ni1+.
